name: Loop 7: Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 7 : Carla-Bayle et le Mas D'Azil — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This loop can easily be transformed into a day of discovery as the possibilities of stops are numerous and interesting. It is one of my favorite routes. The diversity and tranquility of the roads on this side of the Pyrenean Piedmont are dotted with must-sees.

After the charming villages of Carla-Bayle and Montbrun-Bocage, the forest cover becomes denser to the village of Mérigon located at the foot of the Volp gorges. From there, a small departmental road takes you to an unlisted pass at the place called Les Moulis where you will discover a herd of Angora goats.

The dream descent that follows takes you to the intersection with the D 119, which you take to the left until you reach the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil. The place is impressive! To avoid having to take lights, the route passes over the caves. The tunnel is indeed a bit dangerous for bikes.

After Sabarat, a substantial climb on a winding road passes close to an observatory. This is an opportunity to take a last look at these beautiful mountains.

It is by a long flat straight line of about five kilometers, starting at the level of the village of Artigat that you return to your starting point of Fossat.
region_intro: Road cycling around Le Fossat is situated in the heart of the Lèze valley within the Ariège department of Occitanie, France. The region is characterized by its Pyrenean Piedmont setting, offering views of the majestic mountains and rolling countryside. Cyclists will find diverse terrain, from quiet, often traffic-free roads through forests and along rivers, to routes with gentler, rolling, or even flat sections. The area provides a mix of paved surfaces and well-maintained routes suitable for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Le Fossat The most popular road cycling route is Loop 7: Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil – 10 Pyrenean routes, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil, featuring over 1,000 meters of climbing. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Saverdun – Panoramic View of Lescousse loop from Le Fossat, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic views, characteristic of the Pyrenean Piedmont. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Bajou – A Touch of Marrakech loop from Le Fossat, a 20.8 miles (33.4 km) trail leading through rolling countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Road cycling around Le Fossat is defined by its Pyrenean Piedmont setting, quiet roads through forests, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Le Fossat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Le Fossat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Le Fossat?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Le Fossat, offering diverse experiences for various fitness levels. Most routes are moderate, with one challenging option for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Le Fossat?
A: The region offers varied terrain, from quiet, often traffic-free roads through forests and along rivers, to routes with gentler, rolling hills, and even flat sections. You'll find well-maintained paved surfaces, characteristic of the Pyrenean Piedmont.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with substantial climbing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206184206/loop-7-carla-bayle-and-mas-d-azil-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 7: Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.3 km route featuring over 1,000 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find routes with panoramic views of the Pyrenees?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Le Fossat area, situated in the Pyrenean Piedmont, offer stunning views of the majestic mountains and rolling countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6982623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pyrenees – View of Mont Valier loop from Le Fossat&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options or greenways in the area?
A: While the listed road cycling routes are generally moderate to difficult, the wider Ariège department is known for its quiet roads and "Voie Verte" (greenway) paths. These traffic-free routes, often utilizing old railway lines, provide easier, family-friendly cycling options, such as the route between Saint-Girons and Foix.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5305471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carla-Bayle Village&lt;/a&gt; with its Pyrenees views, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pailhes Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2337859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Bordes-sur-Arize&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3103114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills and Countryside East of the Lèze&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Le Fossat?
A: The road cycling routes around Le Fossat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the quiet, traffic-free roads, and the stunning Pyrenean views that provide a picturesque backdrop for their rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Le Fossat?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in Le Fossat. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5350966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saverdun – Panoramic View of Lescousse loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28949336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Bajou – A Touch of Marrakech loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Le Fossat?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Lèze valley specifically?
A: Le Fossat is nestled in the heart of the Lèze valley, so many routes naturally traverse this area. The valley is characterized by verdant hills and pastoral landscapes, offering a tranquil environment for exploration. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38596200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Bascou – Pailhès Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt; will immerse you in the local countryside.

Q: Are the roads generally quiet for cycling around Le Fossat?
A: Yes, the Ariège department, where Le Fossat is located, is known for its quiet, often traffic-free roads. This allows for a peaceful and enjoyable cycling experience through forests and along rivers, away from heavy vehicle traffic.
tips:
This magnificent little village is located on a height. The brick terraces of the southern part of the city overlook the Pyrenees and offer splendid sunsets.

Carla-Bayle owes its name to the French philosopher Pierre Bayle, born in the village in 1647. He was a scholar and a thinker mixing skepticism and rationalism. He is the author of the famous historical and critical dictionary of 1697 which announces the spirit of the Enlightenment. So much so that encyclopaedists will hail Bayle as one of their inspirations.

Here is one of his famous quotes:

"The more we study man, the more we know that pride is his dominant passion, and that it affects greatness even in the saddest misery."

Come stroll through the streets of Carla, you won't regret it.

This beautiful village is at the crossroads between the mountains and the plain. Its weekly market is really famous and attracts a lot of originals and people who live a bit on the margins of society. We thus meet a heterogeneous crowd as well as craftsmen and traders selling quality products.

When the gendarmes arrive to carry out checks, out of solidarity, everyone warns each other. For that, Montbrun makes me think of a village of irreducible Gauls. Apart from Sunday which is lively, there are often deserted streets, but also the best baker in the area.
By this detour, you avoid going through the road that crosses the cavity right through. It is certainly one of the most beautiful views of this unique cave.

By this road (which turns out to have a good percentage of ascent), we again take a little height. The mountains at the bend of very pretty curves on the road and in the landscape appear in all their majesty. This beautiful countryside bears the name of Pyrenean Piedmont.
